name: The passion foretold
notes: Three times along the road to Jerusalem the Lord Jesus foretold His own death and resurrection, and each of the synoptic Gospels records all three foretellings. This weave keeps the three predictions as three clusters, linking the matching details across Matthew, Mark, and Luke pair by pair. The shared core grows fuller each time: the first names the suffering, rejection by the elders and chief priests and scribes, the killing, and the rising the third day; the second adds the betrayal into the hands of men and the disciples' sorrow; the third, on the final ascent to Jerusalem, unfolds the delivering to the Gentiles, the mocking, scourging, spitting, and crucifixion. It is one foretelling given three times, told fuller at each telling, with the three Gospels offering complementary vantages on the same words; where one account keeps a detail another passes over, each still reads whole in its place.
